item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.3 Index, List of Drawings, 1955
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI's CHRONICLE
1955
VOLUME III

LIST OF DRAWINGS

INDEX
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle 1955 Volume III provides a detailed typeset list of drawings across multiple issues and subjects, covering people, places, cultural events, and scenes from around the world such as Macau, London, Tokyo, San Francisco, New York, and Washington DC.

This is the index page for Volume III of Topolski's Chronicle from 1955, listing people, places, topics, and events covered in the biweekly illustrated broadsheet. The page is densely packed with alphabetical references for retrieval, but contains no sketches or handwritten annotations.

pages.typeset_text:
THE DRAWINGS IN “TOPOLSKI’S CHRONICLE” ARE DELIBERATELY SELECTED ONLY FROM THOSE WHICH WERE PUT ON PAPER “FROM LIFE” AND “IN ACTION”; THUS THE AUTHENTICITY OF DOCUMENTATION IS MAINTAINED. IN THE FEW CASES WHEN PAINTINGS OR COMPOSED DRAWINGS ARE INCLUDED, THEIR DIFFERENT NATURE IS STATED

“Topolski’s Chronicle” began in 1953. Earlier collections of pictures by the same artist, which form an almost continuous record from 1935, are:

“The London Spectacle, 1935”, published by John Lane The Bodley Head

“Britain in Peace and War”, published in 1941 by Methuen, London

“Russia in War”, published in 1942 by Methuen, London

“Three Continents”, published in 1945 by Methuen, London

“Portrait of G.B.S.”, published in 1946 by Eyre & Spottiswoode, London and Oxford University Press, New York

“Confessions as a Congress Delegate”, published in 1949 by London Gallery Editions

“88 Pictures”, published in 1951 by Methuen, London

“Sketches of Gandhi”, published in 1953 by The Hindustan Times, New Delhi

SOME ORIGINAL DRAWINGS REPRODUCED IN THE “CHRONICLE” ARE FOR SALE
sketch_descriptions: A dense urban scene showing a busy street with crowds and vehicles, city buildings, and a streetlight.
A sketch of a figure from behind, interacting with a doorway or architectural frame, suggesting a moment of passage or observation.
pages.page_summary: This page serves as an index or reference for drawings and topics covered in Topolski’s Chronicle and related publications, listing subjects, people, and places illustrated by Felix Topolski. It also features two sketches—one of an urban crowd scene and one of a solitary figure—along with publication and documentation notes.
